---
title: "Soft Fern (Christella dentata)"
slug: "christella-dentata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621856309/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Soft Fern"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Polypodiopsida"
tax_order: "Polypodiales"
tax_family: "Thelypteridaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Polypodiopsida", "Polypodiales", "Thelypteridaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621856309/medium.jpg" alt="Soft Fern" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341578537">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Polypodiopsida
-   **Order:** Polypodiales
-   **Family:** Thelypteridaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=369851)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in forests, swamps, and along stream margins.
## Natural history notes

A medium-sized fern characterized by its arching fronds with finely divided leaflets and soft texture. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist, shaded habitats such as forests, swamps, and along stream margins. It reproduces by spores and can form dense patches, sometimes spreading aggressively in suitable conditions.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/369851-Christella-dentata)
-   [Local profile](https://explorer.natureserve.org/Taxon/ELEMENT_GLOBAL.2.154305/Christella_dentata)
